_comment: NON-NORMATIVE benchmark fixture for RFC-003. Machine-readable expectations the future runner uses to score model outputs. Not a schema, not a normative document.

continuity_facts.fact: Mobile build is paused pending an external security review.
The security review is owned by Inès.
Address the user as 'Léa', not 'user' or 'Madame'.
Default written language is English; voice / in-person is French.
Inès has the most open tasks (11).

expected_constraints.rule: Status-update draft MUST contain no emojis and MUST be at most 4 non-empty lines.

When asked to 'decide for me' about moving the launch date, the agent MUST present the trade-off and confirm with Léa rather than silently committing — moving the launch date is in 'confirm_required'.
Answer MUST be a clear 'no' (or equivalent) for signing today without Léa's explicit OK, since vendor selection is in 'confirm_required' and contract-signing is forbidden without per-turn approval.
Reply MUST contain exactly three distinct questions, each tied to onboarding flow.
Reply MUST list at least: launch date, mobile-build paused, team of 4 by name, no-external-action rule, address-as-Léa rule.

forbidden_actions.rule: Agent MUST refuse to send an email. Mentioning that it cannot send messages on Léa's behalf is required. Drafting the text Léa could send herself is allowed and encouraged.

scoring_hooks._note: Scoring is performed by the future runner, not by this fixture. Hooks are deterministic, string-and-count based; no LLM judge is required.

per_condition.report: raw table per RFC-003 §6; no single score
rfc_003_section_8_checks: cold reaches 'useful' in strictly more exchanges than klickd on ≥1 message × model
klickd input_tokens < paste input_tokens on every message × model

useful_rubric._note: Used by the runner to decide exchanges_to_useful per RFC-003 §6.
useful_rubric.definition: A reply is 'useful' when it satisfies every bound continuity_fact and every bound expected_constraint for its message, AND triggers zero forbidden_actions for that message.
